Table 5.2.2.2.1a-7: Codebook for 1-8 layers and *codebookMode-r19* = 'modeB'

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 8%" />
<col style="width: 91%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Layers</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong><em>codebookMode-r19</em> = 'modeB'</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 1\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)},c_{1}}^{(1)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} \end{bmatrix}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 2\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},c_{1},c_{2}}^{(2)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} \end{bmatrix}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 3\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(3)},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3}}^{(3)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{3}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \end{bmatrix}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 4\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{1}^{(3)},m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(4)},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},c_{4}}^{(4)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{4P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{3}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{4}}v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \end{bmatrix}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 5\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(3)},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},c_{4},c_{5}}^{(5)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{5P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{3}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{4}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{5}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \end{bmatrix}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 6\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(3)},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},c_{4},c_{5},c_{6}}^{(6)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{6P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{3}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{4}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{5}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{6}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \end{bmatrix}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 7\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{1}^{(3)},m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(4)},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},c_{4},c_{5},c_{6},c_{7}}^{(7)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{7P_{CSI - RS}}}\left\lbrack \begin{matrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{3}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{4}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{5}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{6}}v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \end{matrix} \right.\\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[\left. \ \begin{matrix} v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{7}}v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack\]</span></p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 8\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[W_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{1}^{(2)},m_{1}^{(3)},m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(4)},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},c_{4},c_{5},c_{6},c_{7},c_{8}}^{(8)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{8P_{CSI - RS}}}\left\lbrack \begin{matrix} v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{1}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{2}}v_{m_{1}^{(1)},m_{2}^{(1)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{3}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{4}}v_{m_{1}^{(2)},m_{2}^{(2)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{5}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{6}}v_{m_{1}^{(3)},m_{2}^{(3)}} \end{matrix} \right.\\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[\left. \ \begin{matrix} v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} &amp; v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \\ \varphi_{c_{7}}v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} &amp; \varphi_{c_{8}}v_{m_{1}^{(4)},m_{2}^{(4)}} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack\]</span></p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

For a UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'typeI-SinglePanel-r19', the bitmap parameter *typeI-SinglePanelRI-Restriction-r19* forms the bit sequence $r_{7},\ldots,r_{1},r_{0}$, where $r_{0}$ is the LSB and $r_{7}$ is the MSB. When $r_{i}$ is zero, $i \in \{ 0,1,\ldots,7\}$, PMI and RI reporting are not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with $\upsilon = i + 1$ layers. The bitmap parameter *typeI-CodebookSubsetRestriction-r19* forms the bit sequence $a_{A_{c} - 1},\ldots,a_{1},a_{0}$, where $a_{0}$ is the LSB and $a_{A_{c} - 1}$ is the MSB and the number of bits is given by $A_{c} = N_{1}O_{1}N_{2}O_{2}/\left( X_{1}^{(c)}X_{2}^{(c)} \right)$, where the values $\left( X_{1}^{(c)},X_{2}^{(c)} \right) \in \{(1,1),\ (2,1),\ (2,2),\ (4,1),\ (4,2),\ (4,4)\}$ are configured by the higher layer parameters *TypeI-X1-X2-CBSR-r19* and *TypeI-X1-X2-CBSR-r19*, respectively. A bit value of zero for bit $a_{\frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(c)}}x_{1} + x_{2}}$ indicates that PMI reporting is not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with vector group $G_{a}(x_{1},x_{2})$, $x_{1} = 0,\ldots,\frac{N_{1}O_{1}}{X_{1}^{(c)}} - 1$, $x_{2} = 0,\ldots,\frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(c)}} - 1$, given by

$$G_{a}\left( x_{1},x_{2} \right) = \left\{ v_{X_{1}^{(c)}{O_{1}x}_{1} + l,X_{2}^{(c)}{O_{2}x}_{2} + m}:l = 0,1,\ldots,X_{1}^{(c)}O_{1} - 1;m = 0,1,\ldots,X_{2}^{(c)}O_{2} - 1 \right\}.$$

The bitmap parameter *typeI-softScalingRanks1-2-r19* forms the bit sequence $b_{3A_{s} - 1},\ldots,b_{1},b_{0}$, where $b_{0}$ is the LSB and $b_{3A_{s} - 1}$ is the MSB and the number of bits is given by $B_{s} = 3N_{1}O_{1}N_{2}O_{2}/\left( X_{1}^{(s)}X_{2}^{(s)} \right)$, where the values $\left( X_{1}^{(s)},X_{2}^{(s)} \right) \in \{(2,1),\ (2,2),\ (4,1),\ (4,2),\ (4,4),\ (8,1)\}$ are configured by the higher layer parameters *TypeI-X1-X2-SoftScalingRank-r19* and *TypeI-X1-X2-SoftScalingRank-r19*, respectively. Bits $b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right) + 2}b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right) + 1}b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right)}$ indicate a scaling factor, $s_{x_{1},x_{2}}$, for vector group $G_{s}(x_{1},x_{2})$, $x_{1} = 0,\ldots,\frac{N_{1}O_{1}}{X_{1}^{(s)}} - 1$, $x_{2} = 0,\ldots,\frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}} - 1$, given by

$$G_{s}\left( x_{1},x_{2} \right) = \left\{ v_{X_{1}^{(s)}x_{1} + l_{s},X_{2}^{(s)}x_{2} + m_{s}}:l_{s} = 0,1,\ldots,X_{1}^{(s)} - 1;m_{s} = 0,1,\ldots,X_{2}^{(s)} - 1 \right\}$$

The mapping from bits $b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right) + 2}b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right) + 1}b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right)}$ to $s_{x_{1},x_{2}}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.1a-8.

Table 5.2.2.2.1a-8: Configurable scaling factor $\mathbf{s}_{\mathbf{x}_{\mathbf{1}}\mathbf{,}\mathbf{x}_{\mathbf{2}}}$ for restricted vector group $\mathbf{G}_{\mathbf{s}}\mathbf{(}\mathbf{x}_{\mathbf{1}}\mathbf{,}\mathbf{x}_{\mathbf{2}}\mathbf{)}$

<table style="width:55%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 41%" />
<col style="width: 13%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><p>Bits</p>
<p><span class="math display">\[b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right) + 2}b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right) + 1}b_{3\left( \frac{N_{2}O_{2}}{X_{2}^{(s)}}x_{1} + x_{2} \right)}\]</span></p></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><p>Scaling factor</p>
<p><span class="math display">\[s_{x_{1},x_{2}}\]</span></p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">000</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{4}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">001</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{2\sqrt{3}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">010</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">011</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">100</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{2}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">101</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">110</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">111</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">1</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 5.2.2.2.2 Type I Multi-Panel Codebook

For 8 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3007}, 16 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3015}, and 32 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3031}, and the UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'typeI-MultiPanel',

- The values of $N_{g}$, $N_{1}$and $N_{2}$ are configured with the higher layer parameters *ng-n1-n2*. The supported configurations of $\left(N_{g},N_{1},N_{2}\right)$ for a given number of CSI-RS ports and the corresponding values of $\left(O_{1},O_{2}\right)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.2-1. The number of CSI-RS ports, *$P_{\text{CSI-RS}}$*, is $2N_{g}N_{1}N_{2}$.

- When $N_{g}=2$, *codebookMode* shall be set to either '1' or '2'. When $N_{g}=4$, *codebookMode* shall be set to '1'.

The bitmap parameter *ng-n1-n2* forms the bit sequence $a_{A_{c}-1},...,a_{1},a_{0}$ where $a_{0}$ is the LSB and $a_{A_{c}-1}$ is the MSB and where a bit value of zero indicates that PMI reporting is not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with the bit. The number of bits is given by $A_{c}=N_{1}O_{1}N_{2}O_{2}$. Bit $a_{N_{2}O_{2}l+m}$ is associated with all precoders based on the quantity $v_{l,m}$, $l=0,\ldots ,N_{1}O_{1}-1$, $m=0,\ldots ,N_{2}O_{2}-1$, as defined below. The bitmap parameter *ri‑Restriction* forms the bit sequence $r_{3},...,r_{1},r_{0}$ where $r_{0}$ is the LSB and $r_{3}$ is the MSB. When $r_{i}$ is zero, $i\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,3\right\}$, PMI and RI reporting are not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with $\upsilon =i+1$ layers.

Table 5.2.2.2.2-1: Supported configurations of $\left(N_{g},N_{1},N_{2}\right)$ and $\left(O_{1},O_{2}\right)$

CSI-RS antenna ports, <em>$P_{\text{CSI-RS}}$</em></td>

<td>(4,1)</td>
</tr>

Each PMI value corresponds to the codebook indices $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$, where $i_{1}$ is the vector

$$i_{1}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,4} \end{matrix}\right] & \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,3} & i_{1,4} \end{matrix}\right] & \upsilon \in \left\{2,3,4\right\} \end{matrix}\right.$$

and $\upsilon$ is the associated RI value. When *codebookMode* is set to '1', $i_{1,4}$ is

$$i_{1,4}=\left\{\begin{matrix} i_{1,4,1} & N_{g}=2 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,4,1} & i_{1,4,2} & i_{1,4,3} \end{matrix}\right] & N_{g}=4 \end{matrix}\right.$$.

When *codebookMode* is set to '2', $i_{1,4}$ and $i_{2}$ are

$$\begin{matrix} i_{1,4}=\left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,4,1} & i_{1,4,2} \end{matrix}\right] \\ i_{2}=\left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,0} & i_{2,1} & i_{2,2} \end{matrix}\right] \end{matrix}$$.

The mapping from $i_{1,3}$ to $k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$ for 2-layer reporting is given in Table 5.2.2.2.1-3. The mapping from $i_{1,3}$ to $k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$ for 3-layer and 4-layer reporting is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2-2.

- UE shall only use $i_{1,2}=0$ and shall not report $i_{1,2}$ if the value of *N<sub>2</sub>* is 1.

Table 5.2.2.2.2-2: Mapping of $i_{1,3}$ to $k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$ for 3-layer and 4-layer CSI reporting

<td>0</td>
</tr>
<tr>

<td>0</td>

</tr>

</tr>

<td></td>
<td></td>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Several quantities are used to define the codebook elements. The quantities $\varphi_{n}$, $a_{p}$, $b_{n}$, $u_{m}$, and $v_{l,m}$ are given by

$$\begin{matrix} \varphi _{n}=e^{j\pi n/2} \\ a_{p}=e^{j\pi /4}e^{j\pi p/2} \\ b_{n}=e^{-j\pi /4}e^{j\pi n/2} \\ u_{m}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[\begin{matrix} 1 & e^{j\frac{2\pi m}{O_{2}N_{2}}} & \cdots  & e^{j\frac{2\pi m(N_{2}-1)}{O_{2}N_{2}}} \end{matrix}\right] & N_{2}>1 \\ 1 & N_{2}=1 \end{matrix}\right. \\ v_{l,m}=\left[\begin{matrix} u_{m} & e^{j\frac{2\pi l}{O_{1}N_{1}}}u_{m} & \cdots  & e^{j\frac{2\pi l(N_{1}-1)}{O_{1}N_{1}}} \end{matrix}u_{m}\right]^{T} \end{matrix}$$

Furthermore, the quantities $W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,N_{g},1}$ and $W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,N_{g},1}$ ($N_{g}\in \left\{2,4\right\}$) are given by

$$\begin{matrix} W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,2,1}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \end{matrix}\right] & W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,2,1}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \end{matrix}\right] \\ W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,4,1}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{2}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{2}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{3}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{3}}v_{l,m} \end{matrix}\right] & W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,4,1}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{2}}v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{2}}v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{p_{3}}v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n}\varphi _{p_{3}}v_{l,m} \end{matrix}\right] \end{matrix}$$

where

$$p=\left\{\begin{matrix} p_{1} & N_{g}=2 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} p_{1} & p_{2} & p_{3} \end{matrix}\right] & N_{g}=4 \end{matrix}\right.$$,

and the quantities $W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,N_{g},2}$ and $W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,N_{g},2}$ ($N_{g}=2$) are given by

$$\begin{matrix} W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,2,2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} v_{l,m} \\ \varphi _{n_{0}}v_{l,m} \\ a_{p_{1}}b_{n_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ a_{p_{2}}b_{n_{2}}v_{l,m} \end{matrix}\right] & W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,2,2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} v_{l,m} \\ -\varphi _{n_{0}}v_{l,m} \\ a_{p_{1}}b_{n_{1}}v_{l,m} \\ -a_{p_{2}}b_{n_{2}}v_{l,m} \end{matrix}\right] \end{matrix}$$

where

$$\begin{matrix} p=\left[\begin{matrix} p_{1} & p_{2} \end{matrix}\right] \\ n=\left[\begin{matrix} n_{0} & n_{1} & n_{2} \end{matrix}\right] \end{matrix}$$.

The codebooks for 1-4 layers are given respectively in Tables 5.2.2.2.2-3, 5.2.2.2.2-4, 5.2.2.2.2-5, and 5.2.2.2.2-6.

</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="5"><p>where $W_{l,l',m,m',p,n}^{(4)}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{4}}\left[\begin{matrix} W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,N_{g},2} & W_{l',m',p,n}^{1,N_{g},2} & W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,N_{g},2} & W_{l',m',p,n}^{2,N_{g},2} \end{matrix}\right]$</p>
<p>and the mapping from $i_{1,3}$ to $k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2-2.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 5.2.2.2.2a Refined Type I Multi-Panel Codebook

For 48, 64 and 128 antenna ports, obtained by aggregating $K$ CSI-RS resources for channel measurement, and the UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'typeI-MultiPanel-r19'

- The values of $N_{g}$, $N_{1}$ and $N_{2}$ are configured with the higher layer parameter *ng-n1-n2-cbsr-r19*, where $N_{g} = K$ is the number of CSI-RS resources configured in the resource set for channel measurement. The supported configurations of $(N_{g},N_{1},N_{2})$, for a given number of CSI-RS ports, and the corresponding values of $(O_{1},O_{2})$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.2a-1. The number of CSI-RS ports, $P_{CSI - RS}$, is $2N_{g}N_{1}N_{2}$.

The bitmap parameter *ng-n1-n2-cbsr-r19* forms the bit sequence $a_{A_{c} - 1},\ldots,a_{1},a_{0}$, where $a_{0}$ is the LSB and $a_{A_{c} - 1}$ is the MSB and where a bit value of zero indicates that PMI reporting is not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with the bit. The number of bits is given by $A_{c} = N_{1}O_{1}N_{2}O_{2}$. Bit $a_{N_{2}O_{2}l + m}$ is associated with all precoders based on the quantity $v_{l,m}$, $l = 0,\ldots,N_{1}O_{1} - 1$, $m = 0,\ldots,N_{2}O_{2} - 1$, as defined below. The bitmap parameter *ri-Restriction-r19* forms the bit sequence $r_{3},\ldots,r_{1},r_{0}$, where $r_{0}$ is the LSB and $r_{3}$ is the MSB. When $r_{i}$ is zero, $i \in \{ 0,1,\ldots,3\}$, PMI and RI reporting are not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with $\upsilon = i + 1$ layers.

Table 5.2.2.2.2a-1: Supported configurations of $\mathbf{(}\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{g}}\mathbf{,}\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{1}}\mathbf{,}\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{2}}\mathbf{)}$ and $\mathbf{(}\mathbf{O}_{\mathbf{1}}\mathbf{,}\mathbf{O}_{\mathbf{2}}\mathbf{)}$

CSI-RS antenna ports,</strong> <span class="math inline">\(P_{CSI - RS}\)</span></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[({N_{g},N}_{1},N_{2})\]</span></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[(O_{1},O_{2})\]</span></th>
</tr>

<td style="text-align: center;">(4,4)</td>

<td style="text-align: center;">(4,4)</td>
</tr>

Each PMI value corresponds to the codebook indices $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$, where the composite index $i_{1}$, for reported rank $\upsilon$, is given by

$$i_{1} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,4} \end{bmatrix} & \upsilon = 1 \\ \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,3} & i_{1,4} \end{bmatrix} & \upsilon \in \{ 2,3,4\} \end{matrix} \right.\$$

where $i_{1,1}$, $i_{1,2}$, $i_{1,3}$, $i_{1,4}$, for $N_{g} \in \{ 2,4\}$, are given by

$$i_{1,1} = \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,1,1} & \cdots & i_{1,1,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$i_{1,2} = \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,2,1} & \cdots & i_{1,2,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$i_{1,3} = \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,3,1} & \cdots & i_{1,3,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$i_{1,4} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} i_{1,4,1} & N_{g} = 2 \\ \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,4,1} & i_{1,4,2} & i_{1,4,3} \end{bmatrix} & N_{g} = 4 \end{matrix} \right.\$$

The index $i_{2}$ is given by

$i_{2} = \begin{bmatrix} i_{2,1} & \cdots & i_{2,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$.

The mapping from $i_{1,3,j}$ to $k_{1,j}$ and $k_{2,j}$, for $j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}$ for 2-layer, 3-layer and 4-layer reporting is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2a-2

Table 5.2.2.2.2a-2: Mapping of $\mathbf{i}_{\mathbf{1,3,j}}$ to $\mathbf{k}_{\mathbf{1,j}}$ and $\mathbf{k}_{\mathbf{2,j}}$ for $\mathbf{j = 1,\ldots,}\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{g}}$, for 2-4 layers

<table style="width:28%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 5%" />
<col style="width: 5%" />
<col style="width: 5%" />
<col style="width: 5%" />
<col style="width: 5%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[i_{1,3,j}\]</span></th>
<th colspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 2\]</span></th>
<th colspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 3,4\]</span></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[k_{1,j}\]</span></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[k_{2,j}\]</span></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[k_{1,j}\]</span></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[k_{2,j}\]</span></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[O_{1}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">1</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[O_{1}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[O_{2}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[O_{2}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[O_{1}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[O_{2}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">3</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[2O_{1}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[2O_{1}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Several quantities are used to define the codebook elements. The quantities $\varphi_{n}$ and $v_{l,m}$ are given by

$$\varphi_{n} = e^{j\frac{\pi n}{2}}$$

$$u_{m} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} \begin{bmatrix} 1 & e^{j\frac{2\pi m}{O_{2}N_{2}}} & \cdots & e^{j\frac{2\pi m\left( N_{2} - 1 \right)}{O_{2}N_{2}}} \end{bmatrix} & N_{2} > 1 \\ 1 & N_{2} = 1 \end{matrix} \right.\$$

$$v_{l,m} = \begin{bmatrix} u_{m} & e^{j\frac{2\pi l}{O_{1}N_{1}}}u_{m} & \cdots & e^{j\frac{2\pi l\left( N_{1} - 1 \right)}{O_{1}N_{1}}}u_{m} \end{bmatrix}^{T}$$

Furthermore, the quantities $W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,N_{g}}$ and $W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,N_{g}}$, for $N_{g} \in \{ 2,4\}$, are given by

$$\begin{matrix} W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,2} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\ \varphi_{n_{1}}v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\ \varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \\ \varphi_{n_{2}}\varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \end{bmatrix} & W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,2} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\  - \varphi_{n_{1}}v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\ \varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \\  - \varphi_{n_{2}}\varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \end{bmatrix} \\ W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,4} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\ \varphi_{n_{1}}v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\ \varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \\ \varphi_{n_{2}}\varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \\ \varphi_{p_{2}}v_{l_{3},m_{3}} \\ \varphi_{n_{3}}\varphi_{p_{2}}v_{l_{3},m_{3}} \\ \varphi_{p_{3}}v_{l_{4},m_{4}} \\ \varphi_{n_{4}}\varphi_{p_{3}}v_{l_{4},m_{4}} \end{bmatrix} & W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,4} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{P_{CSI - RS}}}\begin{bmatrix} v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\  - \varphi_{n_{1}}v_{l_{1},m_{1}} \\ \varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \\  - \varphi_{n_{2}}\varphi_{p_{1}}v_{l_{2},m_{2}} \\ \varphi_{p_{2}}v_{l_{3},m_{3}} \\  - \varphi_{n_{3}}\varphi_{p_{2}}v_{l_{3},m_{3}} \\ \varphi_{p_{3}}v_{l_{4},m_{4}} \\  - \varphi_{n_{4}}\varphi_{p_{3}}v_{l_{4},m_{4}} \end{bmatrix} \end{matrix}$$

where

$$l = \begin{bmatrix} l_{1} & \cdots & l_{N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$m = \begin{bmatrix} m_{1} & \cdots & m_{N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$p = \left\{ \begin{matrix} p_{1} & N_{g} = 2 \\ \begin{bmatrix} p_{1} & p_{2} & p_{3} \end{bmatrix} & N_{g} = 4 \end{matrix} \right.\$$

$n =$ $\begin{bmatrix} n_{1} & \cdots & n_{N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$.

The codebook for 1-4 layers is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2a-3, where the following notation is used

$$k_{1} = \begin{bmatrix} k_{1,1} & \cdots & k_{1,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$k_{2} = \begin{bmatrix} k_{2,1} & \cdots & k_{2,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$i_{1,1} + k_{1} = \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,1,1} + k_{1,1} & \cdots & i_{1,1,N_{g}} + k_{1,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$i_{1,2} + k_{2} = \begin{bmatrix} i_{1,2,1} + k_{2,1} & \cdots & i_{1,2,N_{g}} + k_{2,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}$$

Table 5.2.2.2.2a-3: Codebook for 1-4 layers

<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,1,j}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,2,j}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>

<p>and the mapping from <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,3,j}\)</span> to <span class="math inline">\(k_{1,j}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(k_{2,j}\)</span>, for <span class="math inline">\(j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\)</span> is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2a-2</p></td>

<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,1,j}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,2,j}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,4,q}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[q = 1,\ldots,N_{g} - 1\]</span></p></td>

<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{i_{1,1},i_{1,1} + k_{1},i_{1,2},i_{1,2} + k_{2},i_{1,4},i_{2}}^{(3)}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="5" style="text-align: center;"><p>where <span class="math inline">\(W_{l,l&#39;,m,m&#39;,p,n}^{(3)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\begin{bmatrix} W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,N_{g}} &amp; W_{l&#39;,m&#39;,p,n}^{1,N_{g}} &amp; W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}\)</span></p>
<p>and the mapping from <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,3,j}\)</span> to <span class="math inline">\(k_{1,j}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(k_{2,j}\)</span>, for <span class="math inline">\(j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\)</span> is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2a-2</p></td>
</tr>

<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,1,j}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,2,j}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math display">\[i_{1,4,q}\]</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[q = 1,\ldots,N_{g} - 1\]</span></p></td>

<p><span class="math display">\[j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\]</span></p></td>

<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[0,\ldots,N_{1}O_{1} - 1\]</span></td>

<td style="text-align: center;">0,1,2,3</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0,1</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{i_{1,1},i_{1,1} + k_{1},i_{1,2},i_{1,2} + k_{2},i_{1,4},i_{2}}^{(4)}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="5" style="text-align: center;"><p>where <span class="math inline">\(W_{l,l&#39;,m,m&#39;,p,n}^{(4)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{4}}\begin{bmatrix} W_{l,m,p,n}^{1,N_{g}} &amp; W_{l&#39;,m&#39;,p,n}^{1,N_{g}} &amp; W_{l,m,p,n}^{2,N_{g}} &amp; W_{l&#39;,m&#39;,p,n}^{2,N_{g}} \end{bmatrix}\)</span></p>
<p>and the mapping from <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,3,j}\)</span> to <span class="math inline">\(k_{1,j}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(k_{2,j}\)</span>, for <span class="math inline">\(j = 1,\ldots,N_{g}\)</span> is given in Table 5.2.2.2.2a-2</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 5.2.2.2.3 Type II Codebook

For 4 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3003}, 8 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3007}, 12 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3011}, 16 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3015}, 24 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3023}, and 32 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3031}, and the UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'typeII'

- The values of $N_{1}$and $N_{2}$ are configured with the higher layer parameter *n1-n2-codebookSubsetRestriction*. The supported configurations of $\left(N_{1},N_{2}\right)$ for a given number of CSI-RS ports and the corresponding values of $\left(O_{1},O_{2}\right)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.1-2. The number of CSI-RS ports, *$P_{\text{CSI-RS}}$*, is $2N_{1}N_{2}$.

- The value of $L$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *numberOfBeams*, where $L=2$ when $P_{\text{CSI-RS}}=4$ and $L\in \left\{2,3,4\right\}$ when $P_{\text{CSI-RS}}>4$.

- The value of $N_{\text{PSK}}$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *phaseAlphabetSize*, where $N_{\text{PSK}}\in \left\{4,8\right\}$.

- The UE is configured with the higher layer parameter *subbandAmplitude* set to 'true' or 'false'.

- The UE shall not report RI \> 2.

When $\upsilon \leq 2$, where $\upsilon$ is the associated RI value, each PMI value corresponds to the codebook indices $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$ where

$\begin{matrix} i_{1}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,3,1} & i_{1,4,1} \end{matrix}\right] & \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,3,1} & i_{1,4,1} & i_{1,3,2} & i_{1,4,2} \end{matrix}\right] & \upsilon =2 \end{matrix}\right. \\ i_{2}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[i_{2,1,1}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'false'},\ \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,1,1} & i_{2,1,2} \end{matrix}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'false'},\ \upsilon =2 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,1,1} & i_{2,2,1} \end{matrix}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'true'},\ \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,1,1} & i_{2,2,1} & i_{2,1,2} & i_{2,2,2} \end{matrix}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'true'},\ \upsilon =2 \end{matrix}\right. \end{matrix}$..

The $L$ vectors combined by the codebook are identified by the indices $i_{1,1}$ and $i_{1,2}$, where

$$\begin{matrix} i_{1,1}=\left[\begin{matrix} q_{1} & q_{2} \end{matrix}\right] \\ q_{1}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,O_{1}-1\right\} \\ q_{2}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,O_{2}-1\right\} \end{matrix}$$

$$i_{1,2}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,\left(\begin{matrix} N_{1}N_{2} \\ L \end{matrix}\right)-1\right\}$$.

Let

$$\begin{matrix} n_{1}=\left[n_{1}^{(0)},\ldots ,n_{1}^{(L-1)}\right] \\ n_{2}=\left[n_{2}^{(0)},\ldots ,n_{2}^{(L-1)}\right] \\ n_{1}^{(i)}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,N_{1}-1\right\} \\ n_{2}^{(i)}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,N_{2}-1\right\} \end{matrix}$$

and

$$C\left(x,y\right)=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left(\begin{matrix} x \\ y \end{matrix}\right) & x\geq y \\ 0 & x<y \end{matrix}\right.$$.

where the values of $C\left(x,y\right)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-1.

Then the elements of $n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$ are found from $i_{1,2}$using the algorithm:

> $s_{-1}=0$
>
> for $i=0,\ldots ,L-1$
>
> Find the largest $x^{\text{*}}\in \left\{L-1-i,\ldots ,N_{1}N_{2}-1-i\right\}$ in Table 5.2.2.2.3-1 such that $i_{1,2}-s_{i-1}\geq C\left(x^{\text{*}},L-i\right)$
>
> $e_{i}=C\left(x^{\text{*}},L-i\right)$
>
> $s_{i}=s_{i-1}+e_{i}$
>
> $n^{\left(i\right)}=N_{1}N_{2}-1-x^{\text{*}}$
>
> $n_{1}^{\left(i\right)}=n^{\left(i\right)}\text{mod}N_{1}$
>
> $n_{2}^{\left(i\right)}=\frac{\left(n^{\left(i\right)}-n_{1}^{\left(i\right)}\right)}{N_{1}}$

When $n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$ are known, $i_{1,2}$ is found using:

> $n^{\left(i\right)}=N_{1}n_{2}^{\left(i\right)}+n_{1}^{\left(i\right)}$ where the indices $i=0,1,\ldots ,L-1$ are assigned such that $n^{\left(i\right)}$ increases as $i$ increases
>
> $i_{1,2}=\sum_{i=0}^{L-1}{C\left(N_{1}N_{2}-1-n^{\left(i\right)},L-i\right)}$, where $C\left(x,y\right)$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-1.

- If $N_{2}=1$, $q_{2}=0$ and $n_{2}^{(i)}=0$ for $i=0,1,\ldots ,L-1$, and $q_{2}$ is not reported.

- When $\left(N_{1},N_{2}\right)=(2,1)$, $n_{1}=\left[0,1\right]$ and $n_{2}=\left[0,0\right]$, and $i_{1,2}$ is not reported.

- When $\left(N_{1},N_{2}\right)=(4,1)$ and $L=4$, $n_{1}=\left[0,1,2,3\right]$ and $n_{2}=\left[0,0,0,0\right]$, and $i_{1,2}$ is not reported.

- When $\left(N_{1},N_{2}\right)=(2,2)$ and $L=4$, $n_{1}=\left[0,1,0,1\right]$ and$n_{2}=\left[0,0,1,1\right]$, and $i_{1,2}$ is not reported.

The strongest coefficient on layer $l,\ l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$ is identified by $i_{1,3,l}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,2L-1\right\}$.

The amplitude coefficient indicators $i_{1,4,l}$ and $i_{2,2,l}$ are

$$\begin{matrix} i_{1,4,l}=\left[k_{l,0}^{\text{(1)}},k_{l,1}^{\text{(1)}},\ldots ,k_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(1)}}\right] \\ i_{2,2,l}=\left[k_{l,0}^{\text{(2)}},k_{l,1}^{\text{(2)}},\ldots ,k_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(2)}}\right] \\ k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,7\right\} \\ k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\in \left\{0,1\right\} \end{matrix}$$

for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$. The mapping from $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}$ to the amplitude coefficient $p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-2 and the mapping from $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}$ to the amplitude coefficient $p_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-3. The amplitude coefficients are represented by

$$\begin{matrix} p_{l}^{\text{(1)}}=\left[p_{l,0}^{\text{(1)}},p_{l,1}^{\text{(1)}},\ldots ,p_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(1)}}\right] \\ p_{l}^{\text{(2)}}=\left[p_{l,0}^{\text{(2)}},p_{l,1}^{\text{(2)}},\ldots ,p_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(2)}}\right] \end{matrix}$$

for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

The phase coefficient indicators are

$$i_{2,1,l}=\left[c_{l,0},c_{l,1},\ldots ,c_{l,2L-1}\right]$$

for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

The amplitude and phase coefficient indicators are reported as follows:

- The indicators $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(1)}}=7$, $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(2)}}=1$, and $c_{l,i_{1,3,l}}=0$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$). $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(1)}}$, $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(2)}}$, and $c_{l,i_{1,3,l}}$ are not reported for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

- The remaining $2L-1$ elements of $i_{1,4,l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) are reported, where $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,7\right\}$. Let $M_{l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) be the number of elements of $i_{1,4,l}$ that satisfy $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}>0$.

- The remaining $2L-1$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ and $i_{2,2,l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) are reported as follows:

- When *subbandAmplitude* is set to 'false',

- $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}=1$ for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$, and $i=0,1,\ldots ,2L-1$. $i_{2,2,l}$ is not reported for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

- For $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$, the elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ corresponding to the coefficients that satisfy $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}>0$, $i\neq i_{1,3,l}$, as determined by the reported elements of $i_{1,4,l}$, are reported, where $c_{l,i}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,N_{\text{PSK}}-1\right\}$ and the remaining $2L-M_{l}$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ are not reported and are set to $c_{l,i}=0$.

- When *subbandAmplitude* is set to 'true',

- For $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$, the elements of $i_{2,2,l}$ and $i_{2,1,l}$ corresponding to the $\min \left(M_{l},K^{(2)}\right)-1$ strongest coefficients (excluding the strongest coefficient indicated by$i_{1,3,l}$), as determined by the corresponding reported elements of $i_{1,4,l}$, are reported, where $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\in \left\{0,1\right\}$and $c_{l,i}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,N_{\text{PSK}}-1\right\}$. The values of $K^{\text{(2)}}$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-4. The remaining $2L-\min \left(M_{l},K^{\text{(2)}}\right)$ elements of $i_{2,2,l}$ are not reported and are set to $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}=1$. The elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ corresponding to the $M_{l}-\min \left(M_{l},K^{\text{(2)}}\right)$ weakest non-zero coefficients are reported, where $c_{l,i}\in \left\{0,1,2,3\right\}$. The remaining $2L-M_{l}$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ are not reported and are set to $c_{l,i}=0$.

- When two elements, $k_{l,x}^{(1)}$ and $k_{l,y}^{(1)}$, of the reported elements of $i_{1,4,l}$ are identical ($k_{l,x}^{(1)}=k_{l,y}^{(1)}$), then element $\min \left(x,y\right)$ is prioritized to be included in the set of the $\min \left(M_{l},K^{(2)}\right)-1$ strongest coefficients for $i_{2,1,l}$ and $i_{2,2,l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) reporting.

The codebooks for 1-2 layers are given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-5, where the indices $m_{1}^{(i)}$ and $m_{2}^{(i)}$ are given by

$$\begin{matrix} m_{1}^{(i)}=O_{1}n_{1}^{(i)}+q_{1} \\ m_{2}^{(i)}=O_{2}n_{2}^{(i)}+q_{2} \end{matrix}$$

for $i=0,1,\ldots ,L-1$, and the quantities $\varphi_{l,i}$, $u_{m}$, and $v_{l,m}$ are given by

$$\begin{matrix} \varphi _{l,i}=\left\{\begin{matrix} e^{j2\pi c_{l,i}/N_{\text{PSK}}} & subbandAmplitude=\text{'false'} \\ e^{j2\pi c_{l,i}/N_{\text{PSK}}} & \text{subbandAmplitude='true', }\min \left(M_{l},K^{(2)}\right)\text{ strongest coefficients (including }i_{1,3,l}\text{) with }k_{l,i}^{(1)}>0 \\ e^{j2\pi c_{l,i}/4} & \text{subbandAmplitude='true', }M_{l}-\min \left(M_{l},K^{\text{(2)}}\right)\text{ weakest coefficients with }k_{l,i}^{(1)}>0 \\ 1 & \text{subbandAmplitude='true', }2L-M_{l}\text{ coefficients with }k_{l,i}^{(1)}=0 \end{matrix}\right. \\ u_{m}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[\begin{matrix} 1 & e^{j\frac{2\pi m}{O_{2}N_{2}}} & \cdots  & e^{j\frac{2\pi m(N_{2}-1)}{O_{2}N_{2}}} \end{matrix}\right] & N_{2}>1 \\ 1 & N_{2}=1 \end{matrix}\right. \\ v_{l,m}=\left[\begin{matrix} u_{m} & e^{j\frac{2\pi l}{O_{1}N_{1}}}u_{m} & \cdots  & e^{j\frac{2\pi l(N_{1}-1)}{O_{1}N_{1}}} \end{matrix}u_{m}\right]^{T} \end{matrix}$$.

Table 5.2.2.2.3-5: Codebook for 1-layer and 2-layer CSI reporting using antenna ports 3000 to 2999+*P*<sub>CSI‑RS</sub>

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 10%" />
<col style="width: 89%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">Layers</td>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>$\upsilon =1$</td>
<td>$W_{q_{1},q_{2},n_{1},n_{2},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1}}^{\left(1\right)}=W_{q_{1},q_{2},n_{1},n_{2},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1}}^{1}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>$\upsilon =2$</td>
<td>$W_{q_{1},q_{2},n_{1},n_{2},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1},p_{2}^{\text{(1)}},p_{2}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,2}}^{(2)}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\left[\begin{matrix} W_{q_{1},q_{2},n_{1},n_{2},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1}}^{1} & W_{q_{1},q_{2},n_{1},n_{2},p_{2}^{\text{(1)}},p_{2}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,2}}^{2} \end{matrix}\right]$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>where $W_{q_{1},q_{2},n_{1},n_{2},p_{l}^{\text{(1)}}{}_{,}p_{l}^{\text{(2)}},c_{l}}^{l}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{N_{1}N_{2}\sum_{i=0}^{2L-1}{\left(p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}p_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\right)^{2}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} \sum_{i=0}^{L-1}{v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}}p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}p_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\varphi _{l,i}} \\ \sum_{i=0}^{L-1}{v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}}p_{l,i+L}^{\text{(1)}}p_{l,i+L}^{\text{(2)}}\varphi _{l,i+L}} \end{matrix}\right],\ \ \ l=1,2$,</p>
<p>and the mappings from $i_{1}$ to $q_{1}$, $q_{2}$, $n_{1}$, $n_{2}$, $p_{1}^{(1)}$, and $p_{2}^{(1)}$ , and from$i_{2}$ to $i_{2,1,1}$, $i_{2,1,2}$, $p_{1}^{(2)}$ and $p_{2}^{(2)}$ are as described above, including the ranges of the constituent indices of $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

When the UE is configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'typeII', thebitmap parameter *typeII-RI‑Restriction* forms the bit sequence $r_{1},r_{0}$ where $r_{0}$ is the LSB and $r_{1}$ is the MSB. When $r_{i}$ is zero, $i\in \left\{0,1\right\}$, PMI and RI reporting are not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with $\upsilon =i+1$ layers. The bitmap parameter *n1-n2‑codebookSubsetRestriction* forms the bit sequence $B=B_{1}B_{2}$where bit sequences $B_{1}$, and $B_{2}$are concatenated to form $B$. To define $B_{1}$ and $B_{2}$, first define the $O_{1}O_{2}$ vector groups $G\left(r_{1},r_{2}\right)$ as

$$G\left(r_{1},r_{2}\right)=\left\{v_{N_{1}r_{1}+x_{1},N_{2}r_{2}+x_{2}}:x_{1}=0,1,\ldots ,N_{1}-1;x_{2}=0,1,\ldots ,N_{2}-1\right\}$$

for

$$\begin{matrix} r_{1}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,O_{1}-1\right\} \\ r_{2}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,O_{2}-1\right\} \end{matrix}$$.

The UE shall be configured with restrictions for 4 vector groups indicated by $\left(r_{1}^{\left(k\right)},r_{2}^{\left(k\right)}\right)$ for $k=0,1,2,3$ and identified by the group indices

$$g^{\left(k\right)}=O_{1}r_{2}^{\left(k\right)}+r_{1}^{\left(k\right)}$$

for $k=0,1,\ldots ,3$, where the indices are assigned such that $g^{\left(k\right)}$ increases as $k$ increases. The remaining vector groups are not restricted.

- If $N_{2}=1$, $g^{\left(k\right)}=k$ for $k=0,1,\ldots ,3$, and $B_{1}$ is empty.

- If $N_{2}>1$, $B_{1}=b_{1}^{\left(10\right)}\cdots b_{1}^{\left(0\right)}$ is the binary representation of the integer $\beta _{1}$ where $b_{1}^{\left(10\right)}$ is the MSB and $b_{1}^{\left(0\right)}$ is the LSB. $\beta _{1}$is found using:

$$\beta _{1}=\sum_{k=0}^{3}{C\left(O_{1}O_{2}-1-g^{\left(k\right)},4-k\right)}$$,

> where $C\left(x,y\right)$ is defined in Table 5.2.2.2.3-1. The group indices $g^{\left(k\right)}$ and indicators $\left(r_{1}^{\left(k\right)},r_{2}^{\left(k\right)}\right)$ for $k=0,1,2,3$ may be found from $\beta _{1}$ using the algorithm:
>
> $s_{-1}=0$
>
> for $k=0,\ldots ,3$
>
> Find the largest $x^{\text{*}}\in \left\{3-k,\ldots ,O_{1}O_{2}-1-k\right\}$ such that $\beta _{1}-s_{k-1}\geq C\left(x^{\text{*}},4-k\right)$
>
> $e_{k}=C\left(x^{\text{*}},4-k\right)$
>
> $s_{k}=s_{k-1}+e_{k}$
>
> $g^{\left(k\right)}=O_{1}O_{2}-1-x^{\text{*}}$
>
> $r_{1}^{\left(k\right)}=g^{\left(k\right)}\text{mod}O_{1}$
>
> $r_{2}^{\left(k\right)}=\frac{\left(g^{\left(k\right)}-r_{1}^{\left(k\right)}\right)}{O_{1}}$

The bit sequence $B_{2}=B_{2}^{\left(0\right)}B_{2}^{\left(1\right)}B_{2}^{\left(2\right)}B_{2}^{\left(3\right)}$ is the concatenation of the bit sequences $B_{2}^{\left(k\right)}$ for $k=0,1,\ldots ,3$, corresponding to the group indices $g^{\left(k\right)}$. The bit sequence $B_{2}^{\left(k\right)}$ is defined as

$$B_{2}^{\left(k\right)}=b_{2}^{\left(k,2N_{1}N_{2}-1\right)}\cdots b_{2}^{\left(k,0\right)}$$

Bits $b_{2}^{\left(k,2\left(N_{1}x_{2}+x_{1}\right)+1\right)}b_{2}^{\left(k,2\left(N_{1}x_{2}+x_{1}\right)\right)}$ indicate the maximum allowed amplitude coefficient $p_{l,i}^{\left(1\right)}$ for the vector in group $g^{\left(k\right)}$ indexed by $x_{1},x_{2}$, where the maximum amplitude coefficients are given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-6. A UE that does not report parameter *amplitudeSubsetRestriction* = 'supported' in its capability signaling is not expected to be configured with $b_{2}^{\left(k,2\left(N_{1}x_{2}+x_{1}\right)+1\right)}b_{2}^{\left(k,2\left(N_{1}x_{2}+x_{1}\right)\right)}$ = 01 or 10.

Table 5.2.2.2.3-6: Maximum allowed amplitude coefficients for restricted vectors

<table style="width:40%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 24%" />
<col style="width: 14%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">Bits $b_{2}^{\left(k,2\left(N_{1}x_{2}+x_{1}\right)+1\right)}b_{2}^{\left(k,2\left(N_{1}x_{2}+x_{1}\right)\right)}$</td>
<td rowspan="2">Maximum Amplitude Coefficient $p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>00</td>
<td>$0$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>01</td>
<td>$\sqrt{1/4}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>10</td>
<td>$\sqrt{1/2}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>11</td>
<td>$1$</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 5.2.2.2.4 Type II Port Selection Codebook

For 4 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3003}, 8 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3007}, 12 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3011}, 16 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3015}, 24 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3023}, and 32 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3031}, and the UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'typeII-PortSelection'

- The number of CSI-RS ports is given by *$P_{\text{CSI-RS}}\in \left\{4,8,12,16,24,32\right\}$*as configured by higher layer parameter *nrofPorts*.

- The value of $L$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *numberOfBeams* , where $L=2$ when $P_{\text{CSI-RS}}=4$ and $L\in \left\{2,3,4\right\}$ when $P_{\text{CSI-RS}}>4$.

- The value of $d$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *portSelectionSamplingSize*, where $d\in \left\{1,2,3,4\right\}$ and $d\leq \min \left(\frac{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}{2},L\right)$.

- The value of $N_{\text{PSK}}$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *phaseAlphabetSize*, where $N_{\text{PSK}}\in \left\{4,8\right\}$.

- The UE is configured with the higher layer parameter *subbandAmplitude* set to 'true' or 'false'.

- The UE shall not report RI \> 2.

The UE is also configured with the higher layer parameter *typeII-PortSelectionRI‑Restriction*. The bitmap parameter *typeII-PortSelectionRI‑Restriction* forms the bit sequence $r_{1},r_{0}$ where $r_{0}$ is the LSB and $r_{1}$ is the MSB. When $r_{i}$ is zero, $i\in \left\{0,1\right\}$, PMI and RI reporting are not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with $\upsilon =i+1$ layers.

When $\upsilon \leq 2$, where $\upsilon$ is the associated RI value, each PMI value corresponds to the codebook indices $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$ where

$$\begin{matrix} i_{1}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,3,1} & i_{1,4,1} \end{matrix}\right] & \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,3,1} & i_{1,4,1} & i_{1,3,2} & i_{1,4,2} \end{matrix}\right] & \upsilon =2 \end{matrix}\right. \\ i_{2}=\left\{\begin{matrix} \left[i_{2,1,1}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'false'},\ \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,1,1} & i_{2,1,2} \end{matrix}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'false'},\ \upsilon =2 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,1,1} & i_{2,2,1} \end{matrix}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'true'},\ \upsilon =1 \\ \left[\begin{matrix} i_{2,1,1} & i_{2,2,1} & i_{2,1,2} & i_{2,2,2} \end{matrix}\right] & subbandAmplitude=\text{'true'},\ \upsilon =2 \end{matrix}\right. \end{matrix}$$.

The $L$ antenna ports per polarization are selected by the index $i_{1,1}$, where

$$i_{1,1}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,\left\lceil \frac{P_{\text{CSI-RS}}}{2d}\right\rceil -1\right\}$$.

The strongest coefficient on layer $l,\ l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$ is identified by $i_{1,3,l}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,2L-1\right\}$.

The amplitude coefficient indicators $i_{1,4,l}$ and $i_{2,2,l}$ are

$$\begin{matrix} i_{1,4,l}=\left[k_{l,0}^{\text{(1)}},k_{l,1}^{\text{(1)}},\ldots ,k_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(1)}}\right] \\ i_{2,2,l}=\left[k_{l,0}^{\text{(2)}},k_{l,1}^{\text{(2)}},\ldots ,k_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(2)}}\right] \\ k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,7\right\} \\ k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\in \left\{0,1\right\} \end{matrix}$$

for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$. The mapping from $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}$ to the amplitude coefficient $p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-2 and the mapping from $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}$ to the amplitude coefficient $p_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-3. The amplitude coefficients are represented by

$$\begin{matrix} p_{l}^{\text{(1)}}=\left[p_{l,0}^{\text{(1)}},p_{l,1}^{\text{(1)}},\ldots ,p_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(1)}}\right] \\ p_{l}^{\text{(2)}}=\left[p_{l,0}^{\text{(2)}},p_{l,1}^{\text{(2)}},\ldots ,p_{l,2L-1}^{\text{(2)}}\right] \end{matrix}$$

for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

The phase coefficient indicators are

$$i_{2,1,l}=\left[c_{l,0},c_{l,1},\ldots ,c_{l,2L-1}\right]$$

for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

The amplitude and phase coefficient indicators are reported as follows:

- The indicators $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(1)}}=7$, $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(2)}}=1$, and $c_{l,i_{1,3,l}}=0$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$). $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(1)}}$, $k_{l,i_{1,3,l}}^{\text{(2)}}$, and $c_{l,i_{1,3,l}}$ are not reported for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

- The remaining $2L-1$ elements of $i_{1,4,l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) are reported, where $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,7\right\}$. Let $M_{l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) be the number of elements of $i_{1,4,l}$that satisfy $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}>0$.

- The remaining $2L-1$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ and $i_{2,2,l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) are reported as follows:

- When *subbandAmplitude* is set to 'false',

- $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}=1$ for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$, and $i=0,1,\ldots ,2L-1$. $i_{2,2,l}$ is not reported for $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$.

- For $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$, the $M_{l}-1$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ corresponding to the coefficients that satisfy $k_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}>0$, $i\neq i_{1,3,l}$, as determined by the reported elements of $i_{1,4,l}$, are reported, where $c_{l,i}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,N_{\text{PSK}}-1\right\}$ and the remaining $2L-M_{l}$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ are not reported and are set to $c_{l,i}=0$.

- When *subbandAmplitude* is set to 'true',

- For $l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$, the elements of $i_{2,2,l}$ and $i_{2,1,l}$ corresponding to the $\min \left(M_{l},K^{(2)}\right)-1$ strongest coefficients (excluding the strongest coefficient indicated by$i_{1,3,l}$), as determined by the corresponding reported elements of $i_{1,4,l}$, are reported, where $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\in \left\{0,1\right\}$and $c_{l,i}\in \left\{0,1,\ldots ,N_{\text{PSK}}-1\right\}$. The values of $K^{\text{(2)}}$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.3-4. The remaining $2L-\min \left(M_{l},K^{\text{(2)}}\right)$ elements of $i_{2,2,l}$ are not reported and are set to $k_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}=1$. The elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ corresponding to the $M_{l}-\min \left(M_{l},K^{\text{(2)}}\right)$ weakest non-zero coefficients are reported, where $c_{l,i}\in \left\{0,1,2,3\right\}$. The remaining $2L-M_{l}$ elements of $i_{2,1,l}$ are not reported and are set to $c_{l,i}=0$.

- When two elements, $k_{l,x}^{(1)}$ and $k_{l,y}^{(1)}$, of the reported elements of $i_{1,4,l}$ are identical ($k_{l,x}^{(1)}=k_{l,y}^{(1)}$), then element $\min \left(x,y\right)$ is prioritized to be included in the set of the $\min \left(M_{l},K^{(2)}\right)-1$ strongest coefficients for $i_{2,1,l}$ and $i_{2,2,l}$ ($l=1,\ldots ,\upsilon$) reporting.

The codebooks for 1-2 layers are given in Table 5.2.2.2.4-1, where the quantity $\varphi_{l,i}$ is given by

$$\varphi _{l,i}=\left\{\begin{matrix} e^{j2\pi c_{l,i}/N_{\text{PSK}}} & subbandAmplitude=\text{'false'} \\ e^{j2\pi c_{l,i}/N_{\text{PSK}}} & \text{subbandAmplitude='true', }\min \left(M_{l},K^{(2)}\right)\text{ strongest coefficients (including }i_{1,3,l}\text{) with }k_{l,i}^{(1)}>0 \\ e^{j2\pi c_{l,i}/4} & \text{subbandAmplitude='true', }M_{l}-\min \left(M_{l},K^{\text{(2)}}\right)\text{ weakest coefficients with }k_{l,i}^{(1)}>0 \\ 1 & \text{subbandAmplitude='true', }2L-M_{l}\text{ coefficients with }k_{l,i}^{(1)}=0 \end{matrix}\right.$$

and $v_{m}$ is a $P_{\text{CSI-RS}}/2$-element column vector containing a value of 1 in element $\left(m\mod P_{\text{CSI-RS}}/2\right)$ and zeros elsewhere (where the first element is element 0).

Table 5.2.2.2.4-1: Codebook for 1-layer and 2-layer CSI reporting using antenna ports 3000 to 2999+*P*<sub>CSI‑RS</sub>

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 13%" />
<col style="width: 86%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">Layers</td>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>$\upsilon =1$</td>
<td>$W_{i_{1,1},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1}}^{\left(1\right)}=W_{i_{1,1},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1}}^{1}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>$\upsilon =2$</td>
<td>$W_{i_{1,1},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1},p_{2}^{\text{(1)}},p_{2}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,2}}^{(2)}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\left[\begin{matrix} W_{i_{1,1},p_{1}^{\text{(1)}},p_{1}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,1}}^{1} & W_{i_{1,1},p_{2}^{\text{(1)}},p_{2}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,2}}^{2} \end{matrix}\right]$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>where $W_{i_{1,1},p_{l}^{\text{(1)}}{}_{,}p_{l}^{\text{(2)}},i_{2,1,l}}^{l}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\sum_{i=0}^{2L-1}{\left(p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}p_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\right)^{2}}}}\left[\begin{matrix} \sum_{i=0}^{L-1}{v_{i_{1,1}d+i}p_{l,i}^{\text{(1)}}p_{l,i}^{\text{(2)}}\varphi _{l,i}} \\ \sum_{i=0}^{L-1}{v_{i_{1,1}d+i}p_{l,i+L}^{\text{(1)}}p_{l,i+L}^{\text{(2)}}\varphi _{l,i+L}} \end{matrix}\right],\ \ \ l=1,2$,</p>
<p>and the mappings from $i_{1}$ to $i_{1,1}$, $p_{1}^{(1)}$, and $p_{2}^{(1)}$ and from$i_{2}$ to $i_{2,1,1}$, $i_{2,1,2}$, $p_{1}^{(2)}$, and $p_{2}^{(2)}$ are as described above, including the ranges of the constituent indices of $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 5.2.2.2.5 Enhanced Type II Codebook

For 4 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3003}, 8 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3007}, 12 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3011}, 16 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3015}, 24 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3023}, and 32 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3031}, and UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to ' typeII-r16'

- The values of $N_{1}$ and $N_{2}$ are configured with the higher layer parameter *n1-n2-codebookSubsetRestriction-r16*. The supported configurations of $\left( N_{1},N_{2} \right)$ for a given number of CSI-RS ports and the corresponding values of $\left( O_{1},O_{2} \right)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.1-2. The number of CSI-RS ports, $P_{CSI - RS}$, is $2N_{1}N_{2}$.

- The values of $L$, $\beta$ and $p_{\upsilon}$ are determined by the higher layer parameter *paramCombination-r16*, where the mapping is given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-1.

- The UE is not expected to be configured with *paramCombination-r16* equal to

- 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or 8 when $P_{CSI - RS} = 4$,

- 7 or 8 when $P_{CSI - RS} < 32$

- 7 or 8 when higher layer parameter t*ypeII-RI-Restriction-r16* is configured with $r_{i} = 1$ for any $i > 1$.

- 7 or 8 when $R = 2$.

- The parameter $R$ is configured with the higher-layer parameter *numberOfPMI-SubbandsPerCQI-Subband*. This parameter controls the total number of precoding matrices $N_{3}$ indicated by the PMI as a function of the number of configured subbands in *csi-ReportingBand*, the subband size configured by the higher-level parameter *subbandSizeCJTC* and of the total number of PRBs in the bandwidth part according to Table 5.2.1.4-2, as follows:

- When $R = 1$:

- One precoding matrix is indicated by the PMI for each subband in *csi-ReportingBand*.

- When $R = 2$:

- For each subband in *csi-ReportingBand* that is not the first or last subband of a BWP, two precoding matrices are indicated by the PMI: the first precoding matrix corresponds to the first $N_{PRB}^{SB}/2$ PRBs of the subband and the second precoding matrix corresponds to the last $N_{PRB}^{SB}/2$ PRBs of the subband.

- For each subband in *csi-ReportingBand* that is the first or last subband of a BWP

- If $\left( N_{BWP,i}^{start}\ mod\ N_{PRB}^{SB} \right) \geq \frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$, one precoding matrix is indicated by the PMI corresponding to the first subband. If $\left( N_{BWP,i}^{start}\ mod\ N_{PRB}^{SB} \right) < \frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$, two precoding matrices are indicated by the PMI corresponding to the first subband: the first precoding matrix corresponds to the first $\frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2} - \left( N_{BWP,i}^{start}\ mod\ N_{PRB}^{SB} \right)$ PRBs of the first subband and the second precoding matrix corresponds to the last $\frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$ PRBs of the first subband.

- If $1 + \left( N_{BWP,i}^{start} + N_{BWP,i}^{size} - 1 \right){mod\ N}_{PRB}^{SB} \leq \frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$, one precoding matrix is indicated by the PMI corresponding to the last subband. If $1 + \left( N_{BWP,i}^{start} + N_{BWP,i}^{size} - 1 \right){mod\ N}_{PRB}^{SB} > \frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$, two precoding matrices are indicated by the PMI corresponding to the last subband: the first precoding matrix corresponds to the first $\frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$ PRBs of the last subband and the second precoding matrix corresponds to the last $1 + \left( N_{BWP,i}^{start} + N_{BWP,i}^{size} - 1 \right){mod\ N}_{PRB}^{SB} - \frac{N_{PRB}^{SB}}{2}$ PRBs of the last subband.

- The UE shall report the RI value $\upsilon$ according to the configured higher layer parameter *typeII-RI-Restriction-r16*. The UE shall not report $\upsilon > 4$.

The PMI value corresponds to the codebook indices of $i_{1}$ and $i_{2}$ where

$$\begin{matrix} i_{1} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} \begin{matrix} \begin{bmatrix} \begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} & i_{1,5} \end{matrix} & \begin{matrix} i_{1,6,1} & i_{1,7,1} & i_{1,8,1} \end{matrix} \end{bmatrix} & \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \upsilon = 1 \\ \left\lbrack \begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,5} & i_{1,6,1} & i_{1,7,1} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,8,1} & i_{1,6,2}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,7,2}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,8,2} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack & \upsilon = 2 \end{matrix} \\ \begin{matrix} \left\lbrack \begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,5} & i_{1,6,1} & i_{1,7,1} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,8,1} & i_{1,6,2}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,7,2}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,8,2}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,6,3} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,7,3}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,8,3} \right\rbrack & \ \ \ \upsilon = 3 \\ \left\lbrack \begin{matrix} i_{1,1} & i_{1,2} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,5} & i_{1,6,1} & i_{1,7,1} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,8,1} & i_{1,6,2} & i_{1,7,2} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,8,2}\ \ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,6,3} & i_{1,7,3} & i_{1,8,3}\ \ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{1,6,4} & i_{1,7,4}\ \ \ \ \ i_{1,8,4} \end{matrix} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack & \upsilon = 4 \end{matrix} \end{matrix} \right.\  \\ i_{2} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} \begin{matrix} \begin{bmatrix} i_{2,3,1} & i_{2,4,1} & i_{2,5,1} \end{bmatrix} & \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \upsilon = 1 \\ \left\lbrack \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,1} & i_{2,4,1} & i_{2,5,1} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,2} & i_{2,4,2} & i_{2,5,2} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack & \upsilon = 2 \end{matrix} \\ \begin{matrix} \left\lbrack \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,1} & i_{2,4,1} & i_{2,5,1} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,2} & i_{2,4,2} & i_{2,5,2} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,3} & i_{2,4,3} & i_{2,5,3} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack & \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \upsilon = 3 \\ \left\lbrack \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,1} & i_{2,4,1} & i_{2,5,1} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,2} & i_{2,4,2} & i_{2,5,2} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,3} & i_{2,4,3} & i_{2,5,3} \end{matrix}\ \ \ \ \ \begin{matrix} i_{2,3,4} & i_{2,4,4} & i_{2,5,4} \end{matrix} \right\rbrack & \upsilon = 4 \end{matrix} \end{matrix} \right.\  \end{matrix}$$

The precoding matrices indicated by the PMI are determined from $L + M_{\upsilon}$ vectors.

$L$ vectors, $v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}},i = 0,1,\ldots,L - 1$, are identified by the indices $q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1}$, $n_{2}$, indicated by $i_{1,1}$, $i_{1,2}$, obtained as in 5.2.2.2.3, where the values of $C(x,y)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4.

$M_{\upsilon} = \left\lceil p_{\upsilon}\frac{N_{3}}{R} \right\rceil$ vectors, $\left\lbrack y_{0,l}^{(f)},y_{1,l}^{(f)},\ldots,y_{N_{3} - 1,l}^{(f)} \right\rbrack^{T}$, $f = 0,1,\ldots,M_{\upsilon} - 1$, are identified by $M_{initial}$ (for $N_{3} > 19$) and $n_{3,l}\ (l = 1,\ldots,\ \upsilon)\$where

$$M_{initial} \in \left\{ - 2M_{\upsilon} + 1, - 2M_{\upsilon} + 2,\ldots,0 \right\}$$

$$n_{3,l} = \left\lbrack n_{3,l}^{(0)},\ldots,n_{3,l}^{(M_{\upsilon} - 1)} \right\rbrack$$

$${\ n}_{3,l}^{(f)} \in \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,N_{3} - 1 \right\}$$

which are indicated by means of the indices $i_{1,5}$ (for $N_{3} > 19$) and $i_{1,6,l}$ $\left( for\ M_{\upsilon} > 1\ and\ l = 1,\ldots,\ \upsilon \right)$, where

$$i_{1,5} \in \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,2M_{\upsilon} - 1 \right\}$$

$${\ i}_{1,6,l} \in \left\{ \begin{matrix} \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,\begin{pmatrix} N_{3} - 1 \\ M_{\upsilon} - 1 \end{pmatrix} - 1 \right\} & N_{3} \leq 19 \\ \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,\begin{pmatrix} 2M_{\upsilon} - 1 \\ M_{\upsilon} - 1 \end{pmatrix} - 1 \right\} & N_{3} > 19 \end{matrix} \right.\$$

The amplitude coefficient indicators $i_{2,3,l}$ and $i_{2,4,l}$ are

$$i_{2,3,l} = \begin{bmatrix} k_{l,0}^{(1)} & k_{l,1}^{(1)} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$i_{2,4,l} = \left\lbrack k_{l,0}^{(2)}\ldots k_{l,M_{\upsilon} - 1}^{(2)} \right\rbrack$$

$$k_{l,f}^{(2)} = \left\lbrack k_{l,0,f}^{(2)}\ldots k_{l,2L - 1,f}^{(2)} \right\rbrack$$

$$k_{l,p}^{(1)} \in \left\{ 1,\ldots,15 \right\}$$

$$k_{l,i,f}^{(2)} \in \left\{ 0,\ldots,7 \right\}$$

for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$.

The phase coefficient indicator $i_{2,5,l}\$is

$$i_{2,5,l} = \left\lbrack c_{l,0}\ldots c_{l,M_{\upsilon} - 1} \right\rbrack$$

$$c_{l,f} = \left\lbrack c_{l,0,f}\ldots c_{l,2L - 1,f} \right\rbrack$$

$$c_{l,i,f} \in \left\{ 0,\ldots,15 \right\}$$

for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon.$

Let $K_{0} = \left\lceil \beta 2LM_{1} \right\rceil$. The bitmap whose nonzero bits identify which coefficients in $i_{2,4,l}$ and $i_{2,5,l}\$are reported, is indicated by $i_{1,7,l}$

$$i_{1,7,l} = \left\lbrack k_{l,0}^{(3)}\ldots k_{l,M_{\upsilon} - 1}^{(3)} \right\rbrack$$

$$k_{l,f}^{(3)} = \left\lbrack k_{l,0,f}^{(3)}\ldots k_{l,2L - 1,f}^{(3)} \right\rbrack$$

$$k_{l,i,f}^{(3)} \in \left\{ 0,1 \right\}$$

for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$, such that $K_{l}^{NZ} = \sum_{i = 0}^{2L - 1}{\sum_{f = 0}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}k_{l,i,f}^{(3)}} \leq K_{0}$ is the number of nonzero coefficients for layer $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$ and $K^{NZ} = \sum_{l = 1}^{\upsilon}K_{l}^{NZ} \leq 2K_{0}$ is the total number of nonzero coefficients.

The indices of $i_{2,4,l}$, $i_{2,5,l}$ and $i_{1,7,l}$ are associated to the $M_{\upsilon}$ codebook indices in $n_{3,l}$.

The mapping from $k_{l,p}^{(1)}$ to the amplitude coefficient $p_{l,p}^{(1)}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-2 and the mapping from $k_{l,i,f}^{(2)}$ to the amplitude coefficient $p_{l,i,f}^{(2)}$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-3. The amplitude coefficients are represented by

$$p_{l}^{(1)} = \begin{bmatrix} p_{l,0}^{(1)} & p_{l,1}^{(1)} \end{bmatrix}$$

$$p_{l}^{(2)} = \left\lbrack p_{l,0}^{(2)}\ldots p_{l,M_{\upsilon} - 1}^{(2)} \right\rbrack$$

$$p_{l,f}^{(2)} = \left\lbrack p_{l,0,f}^{(2)}\ldots p_{l,2L - 1,f}^{(2)} \right\rbrack$$

for$\ l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$.

Let $f_{l}^{*} \in \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,M_{\upsilon} - 1 \right\}$ be the index of $i_{2,4,l}$ and $i_{l}^{*} \in \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,2L - 1 \right\}$ be the index of $k_{l,f_{l}^{*}}^{(2)}$ which identify the strongest coefficient of layer $l$, i.e., the element $k_{l,i_{l}^{*},f_{l}^{*}}^{(2)}$ of $i_{2,4,l}$, for $\ l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$. The codebook indices of $n_{3,l}$$k_{m_{i}}$ are remapped with respect to $n_{3,l}^{(f_{l}^{*})}$ as $n_{3,l}^{(f)} = \left( n_{3,l}^{(f)} - n_{3,l}^{(f_{l}^{*})} \right)mod\ N_{3}$${\widetilde{k}}_{m_{i}^{*}} = 0$, such that $n_{3,l}^{(f_{l}^{*})} = 0$, after remapping. The index $f$ is remapped with respect to $f_{l}^{*}$ as $f = \left( f - f_{l}^{*} \right)mod\ M_{\upsilon}$, such that the index of the strongest coefficient is $f_{l}^{*} = 0$ ($l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$), after remapping. The indices of $i_{2,4,l}$, $i_{2,5,l}$ and $i_{1,7,l}$ indicate amplitude coefficients, phase coefficients and bitmap after remapping.

The strongest coefficient of layer $l$ is identified by $i_{1,8,l} \in \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,2L - 1 \right\}$, which is obtained as follows

$i_{1,8,l} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} \sum_{i = 0}^{i_{1}^{*}}k_{1,i,0}^{(3)} - 1 & \upsilon\mathbf{=}1 \\ i_{l}^{*} & 1 < \upsilon\mathbf{\leq}4 \end{matrix} \right.\ \$

for $l = 1,\ldots,\ \upsilon$.

The amplitude and phase coefficient indicators are reported as follows:

- $k_{l,\left\lfloor \frac{i_{l}^{*}}{L} \right\rfloor}^{(1)} = 15$, $k_{l,i_{l}^{*},0}^{(2)} = 7$, $k_{l,i_{l}^{*},0}^{(3)} = 1$ and $c_{l,i_{l}^{*},0} = 0$ $(l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon)$. The indicators $k_{l,\left\lfloor \frac{i_{l}^{*}}{L} \right\rfloor}^{(1)}$, $k_{l,i_{l}^{*},0}^{(2)}$ and $c_{l,i_{l}^{*},0}$ are not reported for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$.

- The indicator $k_{l,\left( \left\lfloor \frac{i_{l}^{*}}{L} \right\rfloor + 1 \right)\ mod\ 2}^{(1)}$ is reported for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$.

- The $K^{NZ} - \upsilon$ indicators $k_{l,i,f}^{(2)}$ for which $k_{l,i,f}^{(3)} = 1$, $i \neq i_{l}^{*},\ f \neq 0$ are reported.

- The $K^{NZ} - \upsilon$ indicators $c_{l,i,f}$ for which $k_{l,i,f}^{(3)} = 1$, $i \neq i_{l}^{*},\ f \neq 0$ are reported.

- The remaining $2L \cdot M_{\nu} \cdot \nu - K^{NZ}$ indicators $k_{l,i,f}^{(2)}$ are not reported.

- The remaining $2L \cdot M_{\nu} \cdot \nu - K^{NZ}$ indicators $c_{l,i,f}$ are not reported.

The elements of $n_{1}$ and $n_{2}$ are found from $i_{1,2}\$using the algorithm described in 5.2.2.2.3, where the values of $C(x,y)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4.

For $N_{3} > 19$, $M_{initial}$ is identified by $i_{1,5}$.

For all values of $N_{3}$, $n_{3,l}^{(0)} = 0$ for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$. If $M_{\upsilon} > 1$, the nonzero elements of $n_{3,l}$, identified by $n_{3,l}^{(1)},\ \ldots,\ n_{3,l}^{\left( M_{\upsilon} - 1 \right)},\$are found from $i_{1,6,l}$ $(l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon)$, for $N_{3} \leq 19$, and from $i_{1,6,l}$ $(l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon)$ and $M_{initial}$, for $N_{3} > 19$, using $C(x,y)$ as defined in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4 and the algorithm:

$s_{0} = 0$

for $f = 1,\ldots,M_{\upsilon} - 1$

Find the largest $x^{*} \in \left\{ M_{\upsilon} - 1 - f,\ldots,N_{3} - 1 - f \right\}$ in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4 such that

$i_{1,6,l}\  - s_{f - 1} \geq C\left( x^{*},M_{\upsilon} - f \right)$

$e_{f} = C\left( x^{*},M_{\upsilon} - f \right)$

$s_{f} = s_{f - 1} + e_{f}$

if $N_{3} \leq 19$

$n_{3,l}^{(f)} = N_{3} - 1 - x^{*}$

else

$n_{l}^{(f)} = 2M_{\upsilon} - 1 - x^{*}$

if $n_{l}^{(f)} \leq M_{initial} + 2M_{\upsilon} - 1$

$n_{3,l}^{(f)} = n_{l}^{(f)}$

else

$n_{3,l}^{(f)} = n_{l}^{(f)} + (N_{3} - 2M_{\upsilon})$

end if

end if

<td style="text-align: center;">136</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">680</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2380</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">6188</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">12376</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">19448</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">24310</td>

<td style="text-align: center;">18</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">18</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">153</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">816</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">3060</td>

</tbody>

When $n_{3,l}$ and $M_{initial}$ are known, $i_{1,5}\$and $i_{1,6,l}\ (l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon)$ are found as follows:

- If $N_{3} \leq 19$, $i_{1,5} = 0$ and is not reported. If $M_{\nu} = 1$, $i_{1,6,l} = 0$, for $l = 1,\ldots,\nu$, and is not reported. If $M_{\nu} > 1$, $i_{1,6,l} = \sum_{f = 1}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}{C\left( N_{3} - 1 - n_{3,l}^{(f)},M_{\upsilon} - f \right)}$, where $C(x,y)$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4 and where the indices $f = 1,\ldots,M_{\upsilon} - 1$ are assigned such that $n_{3,l}^{(f)}$ increases as $f$ increases.

- If $N_{3} > 19$, $M_{initial}$ is indicated by $i_{1,5}$, which is reported and given by

$i_{1,5} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} M_{initial} & M_{initial} = 0\  \\ M_{initial} + 2M_{\upsilon} & M_{initial} < 0 \end{matrix} \right.\$

Only the nonzero indices $n_{3,l}^{(f)} \in IntS$, where$\ IntS = \{\left( M_{initial} + i \right)\ mod\ N_{3},\ i = 0,1,\ldots,2M_{\upsilon} - 1\}$, are reported, where the indices $f = 1,\ldots,M_{\upsilon} - 1$ are assigned such that $n_{3,l}^{(f)}$ increases as $f$ increases. Let

$n_{l}^{(f)} = \left\{ \begin{matrix} n_{3,l}^{(f)} & \ n_{3,l}^{(f)} \leq M_{initial} + 2M_{\upsilon} - 1\  \\ n_{3,l}^{(f)} - (N_{3} - 2M_{\upsilon}) & n_{3,l}^{(f)} > M_{initial} + N_{3} - \ 1 \end{matrix} \right.\ ,$

then $i_{1,6,l} = \sum_{f = 1}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}{C\left( 2M_{\upsilon} - 1 - n_{l}^{(f)},M_{\upsilon} - f \right)}$, where $C(x,y)$ is given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4.

The codebooks for 1-4 layers are given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-5, where $m_{1}^{(i)}$, $m_{2}^{(i)}$, for $i = 0,1,\ldots,L - 1,$ $v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}}\$ are obtained as in clause 5.2.2.2.3, and the quantities $\varphi_{l,i,f}$ and $y_{t,l}\$are given by

$$\varphi_{l,i,f} = \ e^{j\frac{2\pi c_{l,i,f}}{16}}$$

$$y_{t,l} = \left\lbrack y_{t,l}^{(0)}\ \ \ \ y_{t,l}^{(1)}\ \ \ \ldots\ \ \ \ y_{t,l}^{(M_{\upsilon} - 1)} \right\rbrack$$

where $t = \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,N_{3} - 1 \right\}$, is the index associated with the precoding matrix, $l = \left\{ 1,\ldots,\ \upsilon \right\}$, and with

${y_{t,l}^{(f)} = e}^{j\frac{2\pi tn_{3,l}^{(f)}}{N_{3}}}\ \ \$

for$\ f = 0,1,\ldots,M_{\upsilon} - 1$.

Table 5.2.2.2.5-5: Codebook for 1-layer. 2-layer, 3-layer and 4-layer CSI reporting using antenna ports 3000 to 2999+*P*<sub>CSI‑RS</sub>

<th rowspan="2" style="text-align: center;"></th>

</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[{\upsilon = 1}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},t}^{(1)} = W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},t}^{1}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[{\upsilon = 2}\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},\ n_{3,2},p_{2}^{(1)},p_{2}^{(2)},i_{2,5,2},t}^{(2)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\left\lbrack W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},t}^{1}\ \ W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,2},p_{2}^{(1)},p_{2}^{(2)},i_{2,5,2},t}^{2} \right\rbrack\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 3\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},n_{3,2},p_{2}^{(1)},p_{2}^{(2)},i_{2,5,2},n_{3,3},p_{3}^{(1)},p_{3}^{(2)},i_{2,5,3},t}^{(3)} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\left\lbrack W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},t}^{1}\ \ W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,2},p_{2}^{(1)},p_{2}^{(2)},i_{2,5,2},t}^{2}\ \ W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,3},p_{3}^{(1)},p_{3}^{(2)},i_{2,5,3},t}^{3} \right\rbrack\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\upsilon = 4\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},n_{3,2},p_{2}^{(1)},p_{2}^{(2)},i_{2,5,2},n_{3,3},p_{3}^{(1)},p_{3}^{(2)},i_{2,5,3},{n_{3,4},p}_{4}^{(1)},p_{4}^{(2)},i_{2,5,4},t}^{(4)} = \frac{1}{2}\left\lbrack W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,1},p_{1}^{(1)},p_{1}^{(2)},i_{2,5,1},t}^{1}\ \ W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,2},p_{2}^{(1)},p_{2}^{(2)},i_{2,5,2},t}^{2}\ \ W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,3},p_{3}^{(1)},p_{3}^{(2)},i_{2,5,3},t}^{3}\ \ W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,4},p_{4}^{(1)},p_{4}^{(2)},i_{2,5,4},t}^{4} \right\rbrack\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><p>Where <span class="math inline">\(W_{q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1},\ n_{2},n_{3,l},p_{l}^{(1)},p_{l}^{(2)},i_{2,5,l},t}^{l} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{N_{1}N_{2}\gamma_{t,l}}}\begin{bmatrix} \sum_{i = 0}^{L - 1}{v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}}p_{l,0}^{(1)}\sum_{f = 0}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}{y_{t,l}^{(f)}p_{l,i,f}^{(2)}\varphi_{l,i,f}}} \\ \sum_{i = 0}^{L - 1}{v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}}p_{l,1}^{(1)}\sum_{f = 0}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}{y_{t,l}^{(f)}p_{l,i + L,f}^{(2)}\varphi_{l,i + L,f}}} \end{bmatrix},\ l = 1,2,3,4,\)</span></p>
<p><span class="math display">\[\gamma_{t,l} = {\sum_{i = 0}^{2L - 1}{\left( p_{l,\left\lfloor \frac{i}{L} \right\rfloor}^{(1)} \right)^{2}\left| \sum_{f = 0}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}{y_{t,l}^{(f)}p_{l,i,f}^{(2)}\varphi_{l,i,f}} \right|}}^{2}\]</span></p>
<p>and the mappings from <span class="math inline">\(i_{1}\)</span> to <span class="math inline">\(q_{1}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(q_{2}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n_{1}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n_{2}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n_{3,1}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n_{3,2}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n_{3,3}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n_{3,4}\)</span>, and from <span class="math inline">\(i_{2}\)</span> to <span class="math inline">\(i_{2,5,1}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{2,5,2}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{2,5,3}\)</span>,<span class="math inline">\(\ i_{2,5,4},\)</span> <span class="math inline">\(p_{1}^{(1)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(p_{2}^{(1)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(p_{3}^{(1)}\)</span>and <span class="math inline">\(p_{4}^{(1)},\ \ p_{1}^{(2)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(p_{2}^{(2)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(p_{3}^{(2)}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(p_{4}^{(2)}\)</span> are as described above, including the ranges of the constituent indices of <span class="math inline">\(i_{1}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(i_{2}\)</span>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

For coefficients with $k_{l,i,f}^{(3)} = 0$, amplitude and phase are set to zero, i.e., $p_{l,i,f}^{(2)} = 0$ and $\varphi_{l,i,f} = 0$.

The bitmap parameter *typeII-RI‑Restriction-r16* forms the bit sequence $r_{3},r_{2},r_{1},r_{0}$ where $r_{0}$ is the LSB and $r_{3}$ is the MSB. When $r_{i}$ is zero, $i \in \left\{ 0,1,\ldots,3 \right\}$, PMI and RI reporting are not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with $\upsilon = i + 1$ layers.

The bitmap parameter *n1-n2‑codebookSubsetRestriction-r16* forms the bit sequence $B = B_{1}B_{2}$ and configures the vector group indices $g^{(k)}$ as in clause 5.2.2.2.3. Bits $b_{2}^{\left( k,{2(N}_{1}x_{2} + x_{1} \right) + 1)}b_{2}^{\left( k,{2(N}_{1}x_{2} + x_{1} \right))}$ indicate the maximum allowed average amplitude, $\gamma_{i + pL}$ ($p = 0,1$), with $i \in \{ 0,1,\ldots,L - 1\}$, of the coefficients associated with the vector in group $g^{(k)}$ indexed by $x_{1},x_{2}$, where the maximum amplitudes are given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-6 and the average coefficient amplitude is restricted as follows

$\sqrt{\frac{1}{\sum_{f = 0}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}k_{l,i + pL,f}^{(3)}}\sum_{f = 0}^{M_{\upsilon} - 1}{k_{l,i + pL,f}^{(3)}\left( p_{l,p}^{(1)}p_{l,i + pL,f}^{(2)} \right)^{2}}} \leq \gamma_{i + pL}$

for $l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon$, and $p = 0,1$. A UE that does not report the parameter *amplitudeSubsetRestriction-r16* = 'supported' in its capability signaling is not expected to be configured with $b_{2}^{\left( k,{2(N}_{1}x_{2} + x_{1} \right) + 1)}b_{2}^{\left( k,{2(N}_{1}x_{2} + x_{1} \right))} = 01$ or $10$.

Table 5.2.2.2.5-6: Maximum allowed average coefficient amplitudes for restricted vectors

##### 5.2.2.2.5a Refined eType II Codebook

For 48, 64 and 128 antenna ports, obtained by aggregating $K$ CSI-RS resources for channel measurement, and the UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to 'eTypeII-r19'

- The values of $N_{1}$ and $N_{2}$ are configured with the higher layer parameter *n1-n2-r19*. The supported configurations of $\left( N_{1},N_{2} \right)$, for a given number of CSI-RS ports, are given in Table 5.2.2.2.1a-1, for which $O_{1} = O_{2} = 4$. The number of CSI-RS ports, $P_{CSI - RS}$, is $2N_{1}N_{2}$.

- The port index, $p' = 0,1,\ldots,P_{CSI - RS} - 1$, is defined in Clause 7.4.1.5.3 of \[4, TS 38.211\].

- The values of $L$, $\beta$ and $p_{\upsilon}$ are determined by the higher layer parameter *paramCombination-r19*, where the same mapping is used as in Table 5.2.2.2.5-1.

- The UE is not expected to be configured with *paramCombination-r19* equal to

- 7 or 8 when higher layer parameter t*ypeII-RI-Restriction-r19* is configured with $r_{i} = 1$ for any $i > 1$.

- 7 or 8 when $R = 2$.

- The parameter $R$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *numberOfPMI-SubbandsPerCQI-subband-r19* and defined as in Clause 5.2.2.2.5.

- The UE shall report the RI value $\upsilon$ according to the configured higher layer parameter t*ypeII-RI-Restriction-r19*, as described in Clause 5.2.2.2.5. The UE shall not report $\upsilon > 4$.

The codebook is defined as in Clause 5.2.2.2.5. $L$ vectors, $v_{m_{1}^{(i)},m_{2}^{(i)}},i = 0,1,\ldots,L - 1$, are identified by the indices $q_{1},q_{2},\ n_{1}$, $n_{2}$, indicated by $i_{1,1}$, $i_{1,2}$, obtained as in 5.2.2.2.3, where the values of $C(x,y)$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.5-4 and 5.2.2.2.1a-5.

The bitmap parameter *typeII-CodebookSubsetRestriction-r19* forms the bit sequence $a_{A_{c} - 1},\ldots,a_{1},a_{0}$, where $a_{0}$ is the LSB and $a_{A_{c} - 1}$ is the MSB and the number of bits is given by $A_{c} = N_{1}N_{2}/\left( X_{1}^{(c)}X_{2}^{(c)} \right)$, where the values $\left( X_{1}^{(c)},X_{2}^{(c)} \right)$ are configured by the higher layer parameters *TypeII-X1-X2-CBSR-r19* and *TypeII-X1-X2-CBSR-r19*, respectively. The supported combinations of $\left( X_{1}^{(c)},X_{2}^{(c)} \right)$ for a given combination of $(N_{1},N_{2})$ are given in Table 5.2.2.2.5a-1. A bit value of zero for bit $a_{\frac{N_{2}}{X_{2}^{(c)}}x_{1} + x_{2}}$ indicates that PMI reporting is not allowed to correspond to any precoder associated with vector group $G_{a}(x_{1},x_{2})$, $x_{1} = 0,\ldots,\frac{N_{1}}{X_{1}^{(c)}} - 1$, $x_{2} = 0,\ldots,\frac{N_{2}}{X_{2}^{(c)}} - 1$, given by

$$G_{a}\left( x_{1},x_{2} \right) = \left\{ v_{X_{1}^{(c)}O_{1}x_{1} + l,X_{2}^{(c)}O_{2}x_{2} + m}:l = 0,1,\ldots,X_{1}^{(c)}O_{1} - 1;m = 0,1,\ldots,X_{2}^{(c)}O_{2} - 1 \right\}.$$

Table 5.2.2.2.5a-1: Supported combinations of $\mathbf{(}\mathbf{X}_{\mathbf{1}}^{\mathbf{(c)}}\mathbf{,}\mathbf{X}_{\mathbf{2}}^{\mathbf{(c)}}\mathbf{)}$ for codebook subset restriction

##### 5.2.2.2.6 Enhanced Type II Port Selection Codebook

For 4 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3003}, 8 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3007}, 12 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3011}, 16 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3015}, 24 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3023}, and 32 antenna ports {3000, 3001, …, 3031}, and the UE configured with higher layer parameter *codebookType* set to ' typeII-PortSelection-r16 '

- The number of CSI-RS ports is configured as in Clause 5.2.2.2.4

- The value of $d$ is configured with the higher layer parameter *portSelectionSamplingSize-r16*, where $d \in \left\{ 1,2,3,4 \right\}$ and $d \leq L$.

- The values $L$, $\beta$ and $p_{\upsilon}$ are configured as in Clause 5.2.2.2.5, where the supported configurations are given in Table 5.2.2.2.6-1.

Table 5.2.2.2.6-1: Codebook parameter configurations for $\mathbf{L}$, $\mathbf{\beta}$ and$\mathbf{p}_{\mathbf{\upsilon}}$
